title: Working with Lists
instructions: Complete the functions to practice list operations: filtering, transforming, and analyzing data.
hints: Use list comprehensions for filtering and transforming
The 'in' operator checks if an item exists in a list
Empty lists evaluate to False in boolean context

testCases.description: get_evens should return only even numbers from a mixed list
testCases.validation: assert get_evens([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]) == [2, 4, 6], 'Should filter even numbers correctly'

testCases.description: get_squares should return squared values for each number
find_max should return the largest number in the list
testCases.validation: assert find_max([3, 7, 2, 9, 1]) == 9, 'Should find the maximum value'

testCases.description: remove_duplicates should remove duplicates while preserving order
testCases.validation: assert remove_duplicates([1, 2, 2, 3, 1, 4]) == [1, 2, 3, 4], 'Should remove duplicates and preserve order'

testCases.description: find_max should return None for an empty list
testCases.validation: assert find_max([]) is None, 'Should return None for empty list'

testCases.description: get_evens should return an empty list when no even numbers exist
testCases.validation: assert get_evens([1, 3, 5, 7]) == [], 'Should return empty list when no evens'

description: Practice essential list operations in Python by implementing functions for filtering even numbers, squaring values, finding maximums, and removing duplicates. These are fundamental patterns you will use constantly when working with collections of data.
